name: Marcelle Crichton
ai_summary: Marcelle stays calm and regulated during conflicts, separating children to ensure safety and teaching coping skills without shame. She brings 15+ years of diverse childcare experience including substitute teaching, therapy internship, and bilingual children's book authoring, with a nurturing approach that values emotional health and creative, fun learning environments.

transcripts.q3:
Hi, my name is Marcel Crichton. And I've been working with kids since I was about 15 years old. When I was a babysitter, and I worked at a gym childcare. And I also worked as a substitute teacher at a Childcare Center and worked with Children of different ages, all the way from infant up to adolescence and different respects. I did a lot of my internship.
 Europe to become a therapist at a school and I have also worked in schools with kids and I'm providing play therapy for. I visited them in the school setting and become familiar with the teachers and their parents. So I've worked with children for a long time in different ways and I just always have been really passionate about it.
 I have three boys of my own. I have written two children's books, one in Spanish, one and two in English, and one in Spanish, because I'm bilingual. And basically, let's see, I just, I know kids, learn through play. So I really enjoy playing with them and making learning fun, and getting creative.
 Native with it.

So you know, there's that co-regulation there that's huge and assess the situation, you know? Depending if it's if I was there when it happened or if I'm receiving a report from
 Someone. But I would just try to separate them and make sure primary the most important thing, the priority is for them to both be safe. And then, you know, just take them aside and give them some time to cool off and I've taught kids coping skills in the past, so I would do that and, you know, just teach them how to better handle it without shame for anything, but just for them to learn
 Learn how to make better choices in the future and of course, if there was serious enough, I would reported as well to the parents, what the confidentiality for the other child and reported to the administrative and staff as well.
